content:
On Sunday morning, multiple low-flying, slow-moving drones were detected in Finnish airspace over sea areas and Southeast Finland. The Finnish Air Force deployed an F/A-18 Hornet fighter to identify the objects. Two drones subsequently crashed—one north of Kouvola and another east of Kouvola—prompting police to secure both areas for investigation.

Defense Minister Antti Häkkänen confirmed that the drones had strayed into Finnish territory and that security authorities responded immediately. The Defence Forces are currently investigating the incident in cooperation with other authorities.
